object_under_evaluation: ordered repeated-window movement on a bounded workflow surface
claim_boundary: Across this named repeated-window sequence, the repository can read modest directional movement on one anchor workflow surface without turning cleaner reports or one vivid window into general growth.
limitations: This report does not prove general capability growth.
This report does not replace same-task frozen-baseline comparison.
This report does not prove durable movement outside the named window sequence.

windows.workflow_note: Bounded workflow discipline was modest but reviewable on the anchor surface.
windows.context_note: Initial named window establishes the comparison surface before any directional read.

windows.workflow_note: Verification naming and scope discipline improved without major contract drift.
windows.context_note: Reviewer posture stayed comparable and the same anchor workflow surface remained visible.

windows.workflow_note: Summaries became cleaner, but workflow evidence looked mostly flat relative to LG-02.
windows.context_note: Report polish increased more than bounded workflow evidence, so style stayed weaker than movement.

windows.workflow_note: One visible workflow strength from LG-02 disappeared and the final window looked less stable.
windows.context_note: Reviewer mix changed and one case slice refreshed enough to keep the top-line read cautious.
